events.title: Mick Jagger turns 83
events.blurb: The iconic Rolling Stones frontman, Sir Mick Jagger, celebrates his 83rd birthday.

angles.name: Rock Legend
angles.copy: Eighty-three years young and still moving like Jagger! Wishing a very happy birthday to the legendary Sir Mick Jagger. What's your favourite Stones track?

events.title: MTV launched (1981)
events.blurb: Music Television, MTV, launched on this day in 1981, famously kicking off with The Buggles' 'Video Killed The Radio Star'.

angles.name: Video Killed The Radio Star
angles.copy: Forty-five years ago today, MTV burst onto our screens, changing music forever. The first video? The Buggles' 'Video Killed The Radio Star' – a truly prophetic choice!

events.title: Oasis released Definitely Maybe (1994)
events.blurb: Oasis' groundbreaking debut album, 'Definitely Maybe', was released on this day in 1994, propelling them to superstardom.

angles.name: Britpop Classic
angles.copy: Thirty-two years ago, Oasis dropped 'Definitely Maybe' and Britpop was never the same. An absolute essential for any self-respecting music fan. What's your favourite track?

events.title: Woodstock Festival began (1969)
events.blurb: The legendary Woodstock Music & Art Fair, '3 Days of Peace & Music', kicked off on this day in 1969 in Bethel, New York.

angles.name: Peace & Music
angles.copy: Fifty-seven years ago, half a million people gathered for three days of peace and music at Woodstock. A truly iconic moment in cultural history.

events.title: Anniversary of Elvis Presley's death
events.blurb: Remembering the 'King of Rock and Roll', Elvis Presley, who would have been 91 today. He passed away on this day in 1977.

angles.name: Remembering The King
angles.copy: On this day, we remember the King of Rock and Roll, Elvis Presley. He would have been 91 today. His music and legacy continue to inspire generations.

events.title: Anniversary of Tupac Shakur being shot (1996)
events.blurb: On this day in 1996, influential rapper Tupac Shakur was shot in Las Vegas. He would have been 55 this year.

angles.name: Tupac's Legacy
angles.copy: Thirty years ago today, the world of hip-hop was rocked. Remembering Tupac Shakur, a voice that defined a generation, who would have been 55.

events.title: Anniversary of Jimi Hendrix's death (1970)
events.blurb: Remembering guitar legend Jimi Hendrix, who passed away on this day in 1970. He would have been 84 this year.

angles.name: Guitar God
angles.copy: Fifty-six years since we lost a true icon. Remembering the unparalleled talent of Jimi Hendrix, who would have been 84. His music still sounds as revolutionary as ever.

events.title: The Beatles released Abbey Road (1969)
events.blurb: The Beatles' iconic 'Abbey Road' album, featuring that legendary zebra crossing cover, was released on this day in 1969.

angles.name: Iconic Album
angles.copy: Fifty-seven years ago, The Beatles gave us 'Abbey Road'. From 'Come Together' to the epic medley, it's a masterpiece. And that album cover? Timeless.

events.title: Chrissie Hynde turns 75
events.blurb: The formidable frontwoman of The Pretenders, Chrissie Hynde, celebrates her 75th birthday today.

angles.name: Pretenders Icon
angles.copy: Happy 75th birthday to the incredible Chrissie Hynde! Her distinctive voice and rock 'n' roll attitude have given us some unforgettable tracks.

events.title: Liam Gallagher turns 54
events.blurb: The outspoken and iconic former Oasis frontman, Liam Gallagher, celebrates his 54th birthday.

angles.name: Rock 'n' Roll Star
angles.copy: Happy 54th birthday to the one and only Liam Gallagher! Always a 'Rock 'n' Roll Star' and never one to hold back. What's your top Liam moment?

events.title: John Lennon's 'Imagine' released in the UK (1975)
events.blurb: John Lennon's timeless anthem 'Imagine' was released as a single in the UK on this day in 1975.

angles.name: Imagine Peace
angles.copy: Fifty-one years since its US release, but 49 years ago today, John Lennon's powerful message of peace, 'Imagine', was released as a single in the UK. A song that still resonates globally.

events.title: Apple released the first iPod (2001)
events.blurb: Apple unveiled the original iPod on this day in 2001, revolutionising how we carry and listen to our music.

angles.name: Pocket Full of Music
angles.copy: Twenty-five years ago, Apple dropped the first iPod and suddenly, 1,000 songs could fit in your pocket. A game-changer for music lovers everywhere.

events.title: James Bond Day
events.blurb: Celebrating James Bond Day, marking the anniversary of the world premiere of the first 007 film, 'Dr. No', in 1962.

angles.name: Bond, James Bond
angles.copy: Happy James Bond Day! Raise a martini (shaken, not stirred) to 007, the ultimate British spy. What's your favourite Bond film or theme tune?

events.title: John Lennon would have been 86
events.blurb: Remembering the legendary John Lennon, who would have celebrated his 86th birthday today.

angles.name: Lennon's Legacy
angles.copy: On what would have been his 86th birthday, we remember the unparalleled talent and enduring legacy of John Lennon. His music and message continue to inspire.

events.title: John Cleese turns 87
events.blurb: The iconic British comedian, actor, and writer John Cleese, known for Monty Python and Fawlty Towers, celebrates his 87th birthday.

angles.name: Comedy Legend
angles.copy: Wishing a very happy 87th birthday to the brilliant John Cleese! From Python to Fawlty Towers, he's given us some of the most memorable comedy moments.

events.title: The Beatles released 'The White Album' (1968)
events.blurb: The Beatles' experimental double album, officially titled 'The Beatles' but known as 'The White Album', was released on this day in 1968.

angles.name: White Album Wonders
angles.copy: Fifty-eight years ago today, The Beatles unleashed their sprawling 'White Album'. A diverse and groundbreaking double LP that proved they could do anything.
